site.title: Threadbase — Your terminal. In your pocket. Live.
site.description: Threadbase mirrors Claude Code and Codex CLI sessions to your phone in real time. Approve tool calls, redirect the agent, and monitor long runs from anywhere.

betas.title: Threadbase — Beta Programs
betas.description: Join the Threadbase beta on iOS via TestFlight or Android via Google Play. Get early access to the mobile companion for Claude Code.

androidBeta.title: Threadbase — Android beta
androidBeta.description: Join the Threadbase Android closed-alpha beta on Google Play. Two clicks, a short wait, and you're testing the mobile companion to Claude Code.

solutions.title: Threadbase — Other Surfaces
solutions.description: Electron, VS Code, and IntelliJ surfaces for Threadbase. Coming soon.

support.title: Support — Threadbase
support.description: Get help with Threadbase setup, pairing, notifications, bug reports, and app questions.

privacy.title: Privacy — Threadbase
privacy.description: How the Threadbase iOS and macOS apps handle your data.

footer.tagline: Built for developers who don't want to be chained to their desks.
footer.disclaimer: Not affiliated with Anthropic. Claude Code is a product of Anthropic.

hero.headline: Your terminal. Live. In your pocket.

section.heading: Three reasons agent sessions feel trapped in your laptop.

items.title: You're not at your laptop.
items.description: Lunch. Bed. The gym. Walking the dog. An agent is mid-task, blocked on your approval — and you're across the room or across the city.

items.title: Long-running agents waste your day.
items.description: A test suite finishes after 12 minutes. A migration completes. Claude or Codex asks whether to commit. You don't see it until you sit back down.

items.title: Your terminal is glued to one machine.
items.description: The session you started on your iMac doesn't follow you to the MacBook. Your dev environment is wherever you last sat down.

howItWorks.heading: Three steps. Ten seconds. Forever paired.
howItWorks.trustNote: End-to-end encrypted pairing via NaCl. The streamer never talks to a Threadbase server, and it auto-updates itself in the background.

steps.title: Run the streamer on your Mac, Linux box, or Windows PC
steps.description: One install. It runs in the background and exposes your Claude Code and Codex CLI sessions to authorized devices on your network.

steps.title: Pair your phone in 10 seconds
steps.description: Open the Threadbase app, scan a QR code from the streamer. End-to-end encrypted, paired forever.

steps.title: Use your agent from anywhere
steps.description: Your phone is now a live window into your laptop sessions.
steps.postscript: Pair more machines — swipe between them.

section.heading: The phone-shaped tools your laptop was missing.

items.title: Claude + Codex live sessions
items.description: Watch Claude Code or start a new Codex CLI session from your phone, with terminal output streamed character-by-character.

items.title: Remote session control
items.description: Approve tool calls, answer prompts, send follow-ups, or stop a runaway session without getting back to your laptop.

items.title: Multi-server pairing
items.description: Connect one phone to multiple Macs, Linux boxes, or Windows machines. Switch sessions with a swipe.

items.title: Push notifications
items.description: Get pinged when Claude finishes a long task, errors out, or asks a question. Stop refreshing the app to check.

items.title: Native prompt cards
items.description: When Claude asks for a choice or permission, answer with a native card — not by typing into a terminal.

items.title: Resilient by default
items.description: Lose signal in the elevator? Threadbase resumes paused messages and refetches the moment you're back online.

section.heading: Things you should know before you install.

items.title: Closed beta on both platforms
items.description: iOS access is limited while we stabilize — request it via the TestFlight link. Android is in closed testing on Google Play; join the testers group on the Android beta page.

items.title: Your phone and laptop need to reach each other
items.description: Same Wi-Fi network, or a VPN that bridges them. There's no relay server in the middle — that's the point.

items.title: Codex support is newer than Claude support
items.description: Threadbase can read Codex CLI history and start new Codex sessions from mobile. Codex resume and native prompt cards are still being added.

milestones.title: iOS app + macOS/Linux/Windows streamer
milestones.detail: The four surfaces above. iOS via TestFlight, streamer via GitHub Releases on all three desktop OSes.

milestones.title: Cross-session search
milestones.detail: Find that one message from 3 weeks ago across every paired server. Already functional.

milestones.title: Native prompt cards
milestones.detail: Answer Claude's questions and permission gates by tapping a native form — no typing into the PTY.

milestones.title: Android app on Google Play
milestones.detail: Closed testing track. Closing the mobile cross-platform gap.

milestones.title: Homebrew formula for the streamer
milestones.detail: `brew tap RonenMars/threadbase && brew install tb-streamer` replaces the manual GitHub Release download on macOS.

milestones.title: Workspace tagging + saved views
milestones.detail: Tag any session, project, or conversation. Save filter+sort combos as named tabs.

milestones.title: Live Activities + Dynamic Island (iOS)
milestones.detail: Lock-screen session status and Dynamic Island for in-progress sessions.

milestones.title: Scheduled prompts ("send tomorrow at 9am")
milestones.detail: An async-teammate workflow: compose it, schedule it, walk away.

milestones.title: Sync mode + multi-select prompts
milestones.detail: JSONL-sourced message bubbles, plus multi-select support for interactive prompts beyond today's single-choice forms.

milestones.title: Codex CLI live sessions
milestones.detail: Threadbase reads Codex CLI history and can start new Codex sessions from mobile. Resume support is still on the follow-up list.

milestones.title: Durable multi-agent orchestration
milestones.detail: A Temporal-backed pipeline that runs incoming tasks through worker → reviewer → sign-off agent stages, each independently retriable and crash-resilient — no hand-rolled queue.

waitlist.heading: Get notified when it ships.
waitlist.body: One email when multi-assistant support lands. No spam.
waitlist.footnote: No spam. One email when it's ready.

quickStart.heading: Get up and running in under a minute.
steps: # Homebrew service mode also starts on login
# 3. Open the Threadbase app on your phone
#    Tap "Pair" → scan the QR shown in your terminal
#    Run the streamer on your Linux box, Windows PC, or a second Mac.
#    Pair each from the same app. Swipe between sessions.

betas.headline: Join the Beta
betas.intro: Threadbase is available in early access on both iOS and Android. Pick your platform and get the mobile companion for Claude Code on your device.

platforms.name: iOS Beta
platforms.description: The iOS beta is open and ready to install through Apple's TestFlight program. No waiting, no approval — just tap the link on your iPhone or iPad.

steps.title: Install TestFlight
steps.body: If you don't have it already, download TestFlight from the App Store — it's Apple's official beta distribution app.

steps.title: Tap the invite link
steps.body: Open the "Join TestFlight" link above on your iPhone or iPad. TestFlight will open and show the Threadbase beta.

steps.title: Install and launch
steps.body: Tap "Install" in TestFlight. Once installed, open Threadbase and scan the QR code shown by the streamer on your workstation.

platforms.name: Android Beta
platforms.description: The Android beta runs through Google Play's closed-testing program. You'll need to join the testers group first, then opt in via Play — takes about 10 minutes end to end.

steps.title: Join the testers group
steps.body: Open the Google Group linked on the Android beta page and join with the Google account you use on your Android device.

steps.title: Wait ~10 minutes
steps.body: Google's tester-eligibility cache takes a few minutes to update after you join. Grab a coffee.

steps.title: Opt in on Google Play
steps.body: On your Android device, follow the opt-in link on the beta page, tap "Become a tester", then install from the Play Store.

androidBeta.headline: Join the Threadbase Android beta
androidBeta.intro: Google Play's personal-developer policy requires 12 testers over 14 days of closed testing before an app can go live. Help us hit that bar — installation is two clicks and a short wait.

steps.title: Join the testers group
steps.body: Click the button below to open the public Google Group. Sign in to the Google account you want to install on, then click “Join group” (no approval needed).

steps.title: Wait ≈10 minutes
steps.body: Google's tester-eligibility cache needs a few minutes to refresh after you join the group. Grab a coffee.

steps.title: Open the Play Store opt-in link
steps.body: On your Android phone, signed in to the same Google account, tap the second button. Then “Become a tester” → “Download it on Google Play”.

steps.title: Install and (optionally) pair
steps.body: Threadbase will install like any normal Play Store app, marked “(unreviewed)” while we're in closed testing. To use it, run tb-streamer on your workstation and scan its QR code.

fallback.title: If you see “App not available”
fallback.body: Wait another 15 minutes and retry — Play's permission cache occasionally lags. If it persists after 30 minutes, double-check you joined the group with the same Google account you're using on the phone.

androidBeta.closing: Once you're installed, you don't need to do anything else — your install counts toward the 12-tester gate just by staying on your phone.

solutions.heading: Other surfaces
solutions.body: Threadbase also ships Electron, VS Code, and IntelliJ surfaces. The dedicated page for them is coming soon.

support.heading: How can we help?
support.descriptionBeforeEmail: For Threadbase questions, setup help, bug reports, or privacy requests, email
support.contactAfterEmail: . We review incoming support mail directly and will use your email address only to respond to your request.
topics: Questions about installing or pairing the Threadbase app
Help with the desktop streamer, QR pairing, or server connections
Bug reports, crashes, notification problems, or unexpected behavior
Privacy, data handling, and App Store support questions
support.requestIntro: You can send a short message, but these details help us investigate faster:
requestDetails: Your device model and iOS version
The Threadbase app version, if available
What you were trying to do
Whether the issue happens every time or only sometimes
support.privacyBeforeLink: Threadbase is a thin client for self-hosted streamers. The app does not run its own analytics, crash-reporting, or telemetry service. Read the
support.privacyAfterLink: for the full data handling summary.

privacy.heading: Privacy
privacy.description: Threadbase is a thin client for self-hosted Threadbase streamers. It does not run any analytics, crash-reporting, or telemetry service of its own.
staysOnDevice: Server URLs and API keys (Keychain / Keystore via SecureStore)
Session display names, draft prompts, UI settings, quick-access list, React Query cache
All Claude Code session content fetched from your streamer
privacy.leavesHeading: What leaves your device, and where it goes

leavesDevice.data: Session content, prompts, files, status events
leavesDevice.destination: The Threadbase streamer URL you configured
leavesDevice.purpose: Show your sessions in the app

leavesDevice.destination: (a) Expo's push relay; (b) every streamer you've paired with, via POST /api/push/register
The streamer URL encoded in the pair QR you scan

privacy.notCollectStrong: no network calls to any developer-operated backend
privacy.notCollectSuffix: — there is no Threadbase analytics server, no crash-reporting endpoint, no advertising or tracking SDK. Apart from the Expo push relay (used solely to deliver notifications you opted into) and the streamer URLs you yourself enter, the app talks to nothing.

permissions.why: Scan pairing QR codes; attach photos to sessions
Deliver session status alerts from your streamers

yourControl: Remove a server in Settings to revoke its push token and stop all traffic to it
Disable notifications system-wide in iOS Settings → Threadbase
Uninstalling the app deletes every byte stored locally; nothing persists off-device

notFound.heading: Page not found
notFound.body: The route you requested does not exist in this local build.

globalError.heading: Something went wrong
globalError.body: An unexpected error interrupted this route. You can retry the render or return to the homepage.
